judgment.summary: Profitable and growing company with solid margins and a reasonable forward valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 2.3%; next earnings date is 2026-08-12.
positives: Earnings growth 37.1% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 35.4% indicate strong performance.
Profit margin 19.7% and Return on Equity 25.2% demonstrate healthy profitability and efficiency.
Forward P/E of 24.28x is attractive relative to trailing P/E of 38.58x, suggesting growth is priced in.
Free cash flow of $9.29B provides ample resources for operations and shareholder returns.
Analyst consensus is a 'buy' with a mean target price suggesting upside potential.
negatives: Revenue growth of 12.0% YoY, while positive, is lower than earnings growth.
Current ratio of 0.93 and Quick ratio of 0.70 suggest some short-term liquidity constraints.
Total debt of $33.00B is higher than total cash of $16.64B, though Debt/Equity is manageable.
PEG ratio of 1.59 indicates the stock may be slightly overvalued relative to its earnings growth.

news.paragraph: Cisco Systems (CSCO) is highlighted as a top tech holding in Ken Fisher's portfolio, suggesting strong investor conviction despite a recent unrelated tech sector layoff at Intuit. The company recently reported Q3 earnings that beat estimates, with management providing an optimistic outlook for the current fiscal year. The upcoming August earnings date will be a key catalyst to watch for further directional cues.

headlines.title: Impressive Financial Results Assert Cisco Systems, Inc. (CSCO) as a Top Tech Stock in Ken Fisher’s Portfolio
Intuit to Cut 17% of Staff, Invest in ‘Big Bets’
